macro_context.market_sentiment: Bullish into month-end: S&P 500 hit a fresh all-time high at 7,209 (+1.02%), Dow +1.62% to 49,652, driven by strong Caterpillar (+10%) and Eli Lilly (+7%) earnings; investors shrugging off U.S.-Iran geopolitical overhang and Meta's AI capex shock (-9%).
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates at 3.50-3.75% on April 29 in an 8-4 split vote (most dissent since 1992); this was Powell's final meeting as chair — Kevin Warsh takes over in May. Market pricing near-zero probability of cuts in 2026, with inflation elevated at ~3.4% YoY partly from Middle East energy disruption.
macro_context.geopolitical: U.S.-Iran conflict escalated in early March 2026 with Iran declaring Strait of Hormuz closed, restricting ~27% of global maritime oil trade; Goldman Sachs raised Brent forecast to $90/bbl by late 2026. U.S.-Iran peace talks stalled late April. Energy sector is the YTD top performer (+~40%). IMF cut global growth forecast to 3.1% baseline / 2.5% adverse scenario.
key_events: XOM and CVX Q1 2026 earnings both report May 1, 2026 (tomorrow, before market open)
Fed leadership transition: Warsh replaces Powell in May 2026
U.S.-Iran peace talks stalled — Hormuz disruption risk remains elevated
IMF World Economic Outlook April 2026 downgrade — global growth headwinds
U.S. tariff policy uncertainty and potential midterm election impacts in H2 2026

ticker_news.headline: ExxonMobil Q1 2026 earnings due May 1 before open; analysts expect ~39% YoY EPS decline on lower realized commodity prices
Middle East conflict cut ~6% of ExxonMobil's global output in Q1 2026, per Bloomberg report
Golden Pass LNG Train 1 shipped its first export cargo from Sabine Pass on April 22, marking a long-delayed milestone for Exxon's LNG strategy
ExxonMobil in talks to sell Hong Kong fuel-station network for $500-600 million, signaling continued portfolio optimization
XOM stock near record highs in April 2026 after rising 11% in March on energy sector tailwinds from oil price spike

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Wolfe Research downgraded XOM to Peer Perform from Outperform on Apr 21, 2026 (analyst Doug Leggate). Multiple PT raises in April: Wells Fargo to $185, Jefferies to $184, RBC Capital to $180, JP Morgan to $170. TD Cowen maintained Buy but trimmed PT to $172 from $175. 42-analyst consensus: Moderate Buy; range $123-$195; avg ~$162-$165 (current stock ~$154, ~5-8% implied upside).

competitor_activity: Both Chevron (CVX) and ExxonMobil report Q1 2026 earnings on May 1 — results will benchmark sector performance. Analysts slightly favor Chevron for lower capital intensity and stronger FCF durability in a softer oil environment, though XOM has materially higher revenue scale ($82.9B avg quarterly vs. CVX $47.3B). XOM is targeting $25B earnings growth and $35B cash flow growth vs. 2024 levels without increasing capex. BP and Shell face more dividend pressure historically; XOM and CVX both maintained dividends through downturns.
earnings_info: Q1 2026 earnings: May 1, 2026, before market open. EPS estimates range $0.98-$1.80 across aggregators; most cited consensus ~$1.05. Analysts project ~39% YoY EPS decline driven by lower realized oil/gas prices vs. Q1 2025 and Middle East supply disruption impact. Revenue estimate not uniformly cited but consistent with lower commodity price environment. Next quarter (Q2 2026) report expected late July/early August 2026.
